title: Update memory skill content and mandatory guardrail templates
description: Replace append-only markdown memory instructions with `takt memory` usage in managed skill sources and add mandatory read/write or read-only memory sections to the relevant agent guardrail templates.
acceptance_criteria: Worker memory skill content uses `$TAKT_CMD` plus global, feature, and specs searches at bead start.
Operator memory skill content documents `takt memory` retrieval, writing, and migration flows.
Developer/tester/planner templates include mandatory read and write memory instructions.
Documentation/review templates include mandatory read-only memory instructions.
The directly managed `.agents/skills/memory/SKILL.md` is updated alongside source templates used by `takt init`.

handoff_summary.completed: Updated all 5 agent templates with mandatory memory instructions: developer.md, tester.md, and planner.md each have a ## Memory section with three read searches ($TAKT_CMD global/feature/specs) and write instructions scoped to their role (developer/tester: global+feature; planner: global only). documentation.md and review.md have read-only ## Memory sections with the same three searches and an explicit prohibition on writing. The three SKILL.md files were already correctly updated by the prior partial run: worker skills (agents_skills and claude_skills) use $TAKT_CMD with bead-start search instructions; the operator skill (.agents/skills/memory/SKILL.md) documents the full takt memory retrieval, writing, and migration interface.
handoff_summary.risks: Low. These are documentation/template files — no executable code paths changed. The $TAKT_CMD variable is already injected by the orchestrator runner, so the template references are safe.
handoff_summary.next_action: Proceed to tester bead for validation of memory section placement and content correctness across all 5 templates.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None. All changed files are documentation-only templates with no code execution. No file-scope overlap with other in-progress beads expected.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Placed the ## Memory section between Disallowed actions and Expected outputs in most templates for logical flow (agents read memory before working, write after). For review.md the section was placed before Efficiency constraints since review agents are consumers of context before evaluating. Planner's write instruction explicitly notes global-only access to match the access control table in the skill file. Documentation and review templates use an explicit "Do not write to memory" prohibition to make the read-only policy unambiguous rather than relying solely on the access control table in the skill file.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: N/A — no executable code paths were changed. Tester should verify: (1) all 5 templates contain the ## Memory heading; (2) developer/tester/planner have both read (3 searches) and write ($TAKT_CMD memory add) blocks; (3) documentation/review have read blocks and explicit "do not write" prohibition; (4) planner's write block only mentions global namespace; (5) $TAKT_CMD variable references are consistent across all templates.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: N/A — all acceptance criteria are satisfied. The templates use placeholder keywords in the search queries (e.g., "<bead topic keywords>") which is intentional — agents are expected to substitute meaningful terms at runtime.

conflict_risks: None. All changed files are documentation-only templates with no code execution. No file-scope overlap with other in-progress beads expected.

Added mandatory memory sections to all 5 agent guardrail templates. The three SKILL.md files (worker and operator) were already updated by the previous partial run. All 8 expected files now satisfy the acceptance criteria.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
